messages.author: PiGuy9614
messages.text: Hey! Got a great new solution for comprehensive LoRa Mesh network planning. Has countless tools for the three main protocols, along with extensive import & export options for both plans and nodes. It can be 100% locally ran in your web browser OR, for the more technical people, self-hosted as a server that you can access anywhere anytime! I'd love for people to take a look and get some solid use out of it so that we can more effectively expand our mesh! I am working with the lead developer on it, and it is very much a community-driven project. We are very receptive to pull requests, feature requests, bug reports, and even general feedback. Hope to hear from some of you guys soon! https://github.com/PapaSierra555/MeshCommunityPlanner/ (edited)
messages.content_html: Hey! Got a great new solution for comprehensive LoRa Mesh network planning. Has countless tools for the three main protocols, along with extensive import & export options for both plans and nodes. It can be 100% locally ran in your web browser OR, for the more technical people, self-hosted as a server that you can access anywhere anytime! I'd love for people to take a look and get some solid use out of it so that we can more effectively expand our mesh! I am working with the lead developer on it, and it is very much a community-driven project. We are very receptive to pull requests, feature requests, bug reports, and even general feedback. Hope to hear from some of you guys soon! https://github.com/PapaSierra555/MeshCommunityPlanner/ (edited)
embeds_html:
GitHub - PapaSierra555/MeshCommunityPlanner: Desktop application fo...
Desktop application for planning LoRa mesh network deployments with terrain-aware RF propagation, hardware selection, and bill of materials generation. - PapaSierra555/MeshCommunityPlanner
messages.reactions_html: 2

Is it possible to lock a node in place? They are very easy to accidentally move.

messages.author: PiGuy9614
messages.text: Max radius is dynamic based on what the antenna height is set to. If the antenna height is 5 meters for example, it won’t realistically/effectively hit pretty much anywhere 11km away as the crow flies. We were able to increase that dynamically. There is a repeater in my area that is 68meters high, so our radius for that maxed out at 34km. Pretty much just depends on what the radio horizon would realistically be. The lead dev implemented that functionality, so I am not as great at explaining it, but that’s the gist
messages.content_html: Max radius is dynamic based on what the antenna height is set to. If the antenna height is 5 meters for example, it won’t realistically/effectively hit pretty much anywhere 11km away as the crow flies. We were able to increase that dynamically. There is a repeater in my area that is 68meters high, so our radius for that maxed out at 34km. Pretty much just depends on what the radio horizon would realistically be. The lead dev implemented that functionality, so I am not as great at explaining it, but that’s the gist
messages.reply_html:
Clinton
Also, why is the max radius capped at 11 km?

messages.author: PiGuy9614
messages.text: Started working on the node locking feature. Hope to have a PR for that sometime this week. Not completely sure what I can do about the max range situation, as another element to that is processing power limitations (given that this tool does provide a good bit more detail than most open-source "local web app" alternatives). I'll look into the feasibility of an override feature though for that purpose, but it likely would come with negative performance/accuracy implications.
messages.content_html: Started working on the node locking feature. Hope to have a PR for that sometime this week. Not completely sure what I can do about the max range situation, as another element to that is processing power limitations (given that this tool does provide a good bit more detail than most open-source "local web app" alternatives). I'll look into the feasibility of an override feature though for that purpose, but it likely would come with negative performance/accuracy implications.

messages.author: PiGuy9614
messages.text: I see, I think that makes sense. So fleshing this out here, perhaps a checkbox to dynamically set min/max elevation by the screen-bounded region? I guess my only concern with that is that, as I’m sure you have found out, anytime we change the map zoom we have to redownload and reparse all elevation information for that zoom. Adding recalculations to that for new high/low points as well might be a bit rough for performance, but I agree that it would be a nice feature. Same with a tool that puts in high points. Certainly feel free to post a GitHub issue for that if you’d like. I will say, idk if you saw the protocol-specific tools menu yet, but the Meshtastic menu has a tool for suggesting node placement. I’d love to make something like that for MeshCore but with different parameters (particularly altitude and potential coverage being prioritized).
messages.content_html: I see, I think that makes sense. So fleshing this out here, perhaps a checkbox to dynamically set min/max elevation by the screen-bounded region? I guess my only concern with that is that, as I’m sure you have found out, anytime we change the map zoom we have to redownload and reparse all elevation information for that zoom. Adding recalculations to that for new high/low points as well might be a bit rough for performance, but I agree that it would be a nice feature. Same with a tool that puts in high points. Certainly feel free to post a GitHub issue for that if you’d like. I will say, idk if you saw the protocol-specific tools menu yet, but the Meshtastic menu has a tool for suggesting node placement. I’d love to make something like that for MeshCore but with different parameters (particularly altitude and potential coverage being prioritized).

messages.author: Clinton
messages.text: Oh, I did see that there were specific tools, but it never occurred to me to look at the Meshtastic ones. Also, yes, dynamically setting the min/max. Alternatively, if there were a tool to find the local maxima and, heck, local minima, and show the values, that could be useful for 1) knowing what values to use (and perhaps setting them automatically) and 2) knowing how high different areas are.

Alternatively, if there were a tool to find the local maxima and, heck, local minima, and show the values, that could be useful for 1) knowing what values to use (and perhaps setting them automatically) and 2) knowing how high different areas are.
